example: Anyone who's grown up in a traditional Asian family like me knows these mantras well.
They're ingrained in us from an early age and it's an expectation.
These are the foundations of early adulthood and will lead us to happiness.
Even though I checked all of these boxes off, I didn't feel happy or fulfilled.
I landed a competitive corporate job.
zh: 有競爭力的；競爭激烈的

example: Money is something that you can recuperate, regenerate, and rebuild.
Money is something that you can recuperate, regenerate, and rebuild.
What can I do to help me align myself more closely with my values?
This is the story of embarking on my entrepreneurial journey.
I've always been entrepreneurial. I guess you can say it's like an instinct.
I did cold calls and I worked retail.
Although they were pretty mind-numbing, they were giving me real-world experience.
zh: 令人麻木的；枯燥乏味的
ja: 頭がぼんやりするような；退屈な

example: Fast forward three years and I'd started my first side hustle.
An e-commerce dress store offering dropshipping.
ja: ドロップシッピング（在庫を持たずに販売する方法）

example: Now, they all failed miserably but I was undeterred.
ja: くじけない；ひるまない

example: I kept myself busy by brainstorming ideas to new businesses.
ja: ブレインストーミング

example: I graduated university and began working my nine-to-five corporate job.
The routine and ritual of the corporate world was driving me down.
It was driving me down into a state of habitual coma.
zh: 習慣性昏睡狀態（比喻極度無聊或麻木）
ja: 習慣的な昏睡状態（比喩的表現）

example: I felt trapped and I had a feeling of uneasiness.
ja: 不安；落ち着かない感じ

example: I yearned for the freedom that would allow me the opportunity to choose my own schedule.
I decided to start another entrepreneurial venture.
ja: ベンチャー；冒険的事業

example: We found our niche and our business idea was born.
There weren't many bikini brands that flattered petite bodies.
I handled the manufacturing side, the operations and the marketing.
I worked after hours when I finished the daily grind of my corporate job.
We also had a brick and mortar store for training.
By this point, I was building financial freedom.
My two side hustles were growing exponentially.
I didn't stroll out of the office that day on Cloud9.
My corporate job was my only backup if these businesses failed.
That type of thinking had kept me in the rat race for too long.
I have a lot of mentors who have guided me through my journey.
Role models who inspire me.
Have faith in yourself and hang in there.
Celebrate and appreciate the wins.
Set small, achievable goals.
You can hold yourself accountable and stay focused on making progress.
Remember, consistency is key.
I want to continue mentoring young women.
Help them achieve their entrepreneurship goals.
The freedom of working for yourself is truly priceless.
Use each setback as a learning lesson.
Don't underestimate yourself.
Your entrepreneurial journey may be an uphill battle.
Don't forget to stop and savor the moment.
